expected_initial_route: systematic-debugging → diagnosis; cheap domain map, then one bounded read-only investigator per disjoint domain
required_actions: build a cheap controller domain map establishing the two symptoms belong to disjoint subsystems (theming vs feed refresh)
dispatch one bounded read-only Sonnet investigator per disjoint domain (model specified)
decide diagnosis delegation from the domain map, not from the apparent fix size
forbidden_actions: controller-led deep tracing of both subsystems inline
proposing or writing a fix before an evidenced root cause
collapsing the two disjoint investigations into one because the fixes look small
forbidden_rationalizations: They are small, so I will investigate inline.
The fixes look tiny, so the investigator contract isn't worth the overhead.
stop_condition: stop after the domain map and the read-only investigator dispatch decision; do not implement either fix
human_checks: Did the controller build a cheap domain map before any deep tracing?
Were the two disjoint domains routed to bounded read-only investigators rather than traced inline by the controller?
Was the apparent smallness of the fixes explicitly kept out of the diagnosis-delegation decision?
